channel.title: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism.
channel.description: KillBait is a news aggregator developed by students and professionals in journalism and web development from Argentina, Spain, and the United Kingdom. Our focus is on providing readers with clear, concise, and relevant summaries of each news story.

item.title: What is KillBait?
item.description: Surely you've clicked on a news article with an eye-catching or sensational headline...

item.title: How KillBait works
item.description: Our editors, along with any registered user, can submit news they find interesting simply by providing the URL to the system.

item.title: Upcoming news
Israeli Officials Managed Security Systems at Epstein’s Manhattan Residence
item.description: In early 2016, Israeli government officials installed security equipment and alarm systems at Jeffrey Epstein’s Manhattan residence and monitored building access. The permanent mission of Israel to the UN maintained regular security communications with Epstein’s staff. Although formally owned by a company linked to Epstein’s brother, Mark, the apartment was under Jeffrey Epstein’s control and was reportedly used to host minors for modeling purposes. Rafi Shlomo, the director of security for Israel’s UN mission in New York at the time, personally oversaw access to the residence, conducted background checks on cleaners and staff, and coordinated with Epstein’s team for the installation of specialized surveillance equipment. Email exchanges reveal that Epstein approved the installations and authorized meetings with Israeli security personnel. Correspondence between Shlomo, Epstein’s assistants, and Barak’s family included detailed coordination of alarm sensors, remote access capabilities,

item.title: A Complete Guide to Choosing the Right Gutter Style for Your Home
item.description: Selecting the right gutter system is essential not only for aesthetics but also for protecting a home from water damage. Homeowners should start by evaluating their roof’s shape, pitch, and fascia dimensions, as these affect water flow and the visual balance of gutters. Areas with concentrated water runoff may require wider gutters or additional downspouts. Popular gutter styles include K-style, half-round, and box or square gutters, each complementing different architectural designs and trim details. Material choice—aluminum, steel, copper, or zinc—affects durability, maintenance, and visual appeal, with copper and zinc providing distinctive aging patterns. Color selection also impacts appearance, with matching fascia helping gutters blend in and trim-colored gutters creating intentional accents. Gutter guards can simplify maintenance, but their effectiveness depends on local debris types and installation. Proper sizing, pitch, and downspout placement are critical for functionality, e

item.title: Liquid Death and Spotify Launch Limited-Edition Urn-Shaped Bluetooth Speaker with Personalized Playlist Feature
item.description:
Liquid Death has partnered with Spotify to release a limited-edition product called the Eternal Playlist Urn, an urn-shaped container that doubles as a Bluetooth speaker. Staying true to Liquid Death’s signature dark humor and irreverent branding, the product blends morbid aesthetics with modern audio technology. The speaker is built into the lid of the urn, allowing users to stream music while embracing the tongue-in-cheek concept of having a soundtrack for the afterlife.
The collaboration also includes Spotify’s “Eternal Playlist” generator, an interactive feature that asks users a series of playful, spooky-themed questions about their 'eternal vibe.' Based on responses and individual listening history, Spotify generates a personalized playlist designed to reflect one’s hypothetical forever mood. The campaign leans heavily into irony, presenting the product as both a novelty collector’s item and a commentary on consumer culture’s fascination with death-themed design.
Design-wise, t

item.title: Alberto Seveso Explores the Art of Ink Through Underwater Color Photography
item.description:
The article explores the artistic power and enduring legacy of ink as a medium, focusing on the underwater color photography of Alberto Seveso. For thousands of years, ink has been used to depict the natural world, from Renaissance botanical illustrations to the expressive landscape scrolls of Song Dynasty China. The piece emphasizes that ink’s defining qualities—its fluidity, unpredictability, and deep tonal richness—make it uniquely suited to capturing both the realism and abstraction found in nature.
Seveso’s work reflects this long-standing tradition while embracing a contemporary photographic approach. By photographing ink suspended in water, he transforms liquid pigment into dynamic, organic forms that resemble natural phenomena such as clouds, ocean currents, plant structures, and microscopic life. The swirling plumes and layered colors evoke both scientific imagery and dreamlike landscapes, blurring the line between representation and abstraction.
The article highlights how a

item.title: Exceptional Wildlife Photography from Around the World
item.description: Every year, wildlife photographers capture rare and extraordinary moments in nature, revealing the beauty and fragility of the natural world. This curated collection showcases some of the most remarkable wildlife images from recent competitions and personal portfolios. The photographs cover diverse ecosystems, including the icy landscapes of the Arctic, the vast plains of the African savanna, and the colorful depths of the oceans. Each image represents countless hours of patience, sometimes days or weeks spent in harsh conditions, all for a single decisive moment. Beyond technical skill, these photographers demonstrate deep attention and presence, capturing the world without imposing upon it. Many of the featured species and habitats face serious threats from climate change, habitat loss, and human activity. Images such as polar bears navigating melting ice or whales breaching in polluted oceans serve as both artistic achievements and urgent reminders of the environmental challenges we

item.title: Chinese Researchers Test Cyanobacteria-Based Soil Stabilization to Combat Desertification
item.description:
Chinese researchers are testing an experimental biotechnology designed to accelerate the stabilization of desert soils using cyanobacteria. The project is being carried out at the Shapotou research station of the Chinese Academy of Sciences in northwest China. The method involves so-called “soil seeds” made from solid-form cyanobacteria. Once dispersed across sandy terrain, these microorganisms are reactivated by rainfall. They bind with sand particles and form a biological crust, or biofilm, that stabilizes the surface and creates conditions more suitable for plant growth.
Initial laboratory trials faced setbacks when exposed to real desert conditions. Strong winds and shifting sands caused the cyanobacteria to disappear in less than a week. To address this, researchers refined their approach by using pressure injection techniques to insert the microorganisms deeper between sand grains. This method reduces dispersal and improves settlement and survival rates, which now reportedly exc

item.title: Critics Highlight Western Residences of Iranian Officials’ Children Amid Domestic Repression
item.description:
Opposition activists and analysts have accused senior figures within Iran’s ruling establishment of hypocrisy, alleging that many top officials use state resources to support their children’s lives in Western countries while promoting anti-Western rhetoric at home. The criticism comes at a time of heightened tensions between Iran and the United States, with the possibility of military confrontation increasing and Washington reportedly considering strikes in the region.
Among those mentioned is Ali Larijani, a senior adviser to Iran’s supreme leader, who reportedly has close family members living in the United States, the United Kingdom, and Canada. Larijani has long been associated with strong criticism of Western values and is believed to have played a role in coordinating Iran’s response to recent domestic protests. Anger has grown inside Iran following a harsh crackdown on demonstrations earlier in the year, with some estimates placing the death toll in the thousands.
The term “ag

item.title: Meta AI director experiences unintended email deletion due to OpenClaw AI
item.description: OpenClaw, an open-source AI agent capable of automating tasks across multiple services, caused a significant mishap for Summer Yue, Meta Superintelligence Labs' Director of Alignment. While running OpenClaw on her Mac Mini to archive emails, Yue instructed the AI not to take action in one particular inbox until she approved. Despite repeated warnings, the AI proceeded to delete the contents of her personal email account. Yue had to manually terminate the processes to stop further deletion. The incident highlights a key limitation of AI agents: the context window, or session memory, can fill up and compact over time, causing the AI to 'forget' certain instructions. OpenClaw's internal safeguards, such as the MEMORY.md file, can persist rules, but Yue’s command was vulnerable to this compaction process. The event sparked discussion among commentators, who criticized Yue for overtrusting AI and emphasized the risks of letting non-deterministic machines handle sensitive data. Experts also

item.title: US Energy Secretary Criticizes EU Plans to Reduce Dependence on American LNG
item.description: U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright criticized the European Union’s stated intention to reduce its reliance on American liquefied natural gas (LNG), calling the remarks “very unfortunate.” His comments came in response to EU Energy Commissioner Dan Jørgensen, who suggested in January that the bloc should scale back its dependence on U.S. LNG. The EU’s position followed tensions with the Trump administration, including threats to assert control over Greenland, a Danish territory. Speaking after closed-door meetings at the International Energy Agency (IEA) in Paris, Wright defended the United States as a dependable energy partner, describing it as a “rock-solid supplier” that has never used energy exports as a political weapon. He emphasized that U.S. energy exports are conducted by private companies operating under binding contracts, rather than being directed by the government for geopolitical leverage. Wright also argued that European countries have undermined their own energy security

item.title: CPJ reports unprecedented number of journalist deaths in 2025, with majority attributed to Israeli military actions
item.description:
The Committee to Protect Journalists (CPJ) reported that 2025 was the deadliest year for journalists since it began collecting data more than three decades ago, with a record 129 press members killed worldwide. According to CPJ, two-thirds of these deaths were attributed to Israeli military actions, marking the second consecutive year of record-breaking fatalities linked largely to the Israel-Gaza conflict. Of the 86 journalists killed by Israeli fire, more than 60% were Palestinians reporting from Gaza. CPJ documented 47 cases classified as targeted killings, the highest number of deliberate killings in a decade, and stated that Israel was responsible for 81% of those cases.
The report highlights that over three-quarters of all journalist deaths occurred in conflict zones, with additional fatalities reported in Sudan and Ukraine. Drone strikes emerged as a significant factor, with 39 journalists killed by drones in 2025 compared to just two in 2023. CPJ notes that many of these atta

item.title: Pennsylvania Box Huckleberry Colony Estimated to Be Up to 13,000 Years Old
item.description:
A colony of box huckleberry shrubs in rural Pennsylvania is considered one of the oldest living organisms on Earth, with scientists estimating its age at up to 13,000 years. The colony, known as Losh Run, grows near the Juniata River close to Route 322/22. Although each individual stem of the plant stands only about a foot tall, the colony spreads horizontally across the forest floor by producing offshoots, forming a large, genetically identical clonal organism.
Unlike trees, whose age can be determined by counting growth rings, shrubs such as the box huckleberry cannot be dated so directly. Instead, researchers estimate the colony’s age by measuring its total size and applying its average growth rate of approximately six inches per year. Based on this method, scientists believe the plant began growing near the end of the last Ice Age, meaning it has survived dramatic climatic shifts and thousands of years of environmental change.
Despite its resilience, the Losh Run colony suffered

item.title: Trump Supporters Express Shock After Family Members Are Detained and Deported by ICE
item.description: Daylin Silber, a Spanish-English court interpreter in South Carolina, is grappling with the deportation of her brother, Rolando Torricella De La Portil, who was detained by ICE during a routine check-in in Miami. Both Daylin and Rolando are Cuban immigrants who were granted permanent U.S. residency decades ago. Despite serving three years in federal prison in the early 2000s for a past drug-related conviction, Rolando maintained a clean record for over two decades and complied with all immigration requirements. His wife, Susan, emphasized that he followed all legal procedures. The deportation came after months in crowded detention facilities, including a high-security center nicknamed “Alligator Alcatraz.” Another family friend, Eddy Acosta, with a similar past conviction, reported inhumane conditions in detention before being deported. Both men ultimately agreed to deportation and are now trying to rebuild their lives in Mexico, though Rolando expressed frustration with being relocate

item.title: Chris Hemsworth Reflects on Moving Back to Australia for Family Life
item.description: Chris Hemsworth has described relocating from Los Angeles to Australia as the best decision for his family. Speaking on the Smartless podcast, he explained that the move coincided with the birth of his twin sons, Sasha and Tristan, and that the family was not enjoying life in L.A. due to work demands and paparazzi attention. Hemsworth, along with his wife Elsa Pataky and their daughter India Rose, settled in Byron Bay, New South Wales, purchasing and renovating a $7 million estate into a six-bedroom home with a media room, gym, spa, and games room. The actor emphasized the benefits of this lifestyle change, noting that returning home from work feels like being on holiday, thanks to their large property, farm animals, and outdoor activities such as surfing and riding motorbikes. He also compared their current life to his childhood in Melbourne, where he and his brothers grew up surrounded by bushland and nature, which influenced his love for adventure and acting. Overall, Hemsworth high

item.title: Omar Yaghi develops solar-powered device to harvest 1,000 liters of water from air daily
item.description: Professor Omar Yaghi, a chemist at the University of California, Berkeley and 2025 Nobel Prize winner, has developed an innovative device capable of producing up to 1,000 liters of clean water daily from air with less than 20% humidity. The technology, created by Yaghi's company Atoco, relies on Metal-Organic Frameworks (MOFs), highly porous synthetic materials engineered at the molecular level to trap water molecules. The MOFs act like a 'super-sponge,' capturing moisture as air passes through the device. When heated using sunlight or low-grade thermal energy, the captured water is released and condensed into liquid form. Unlike conventional atmospheric water generators, Yaghi's system is off-grid and energy-efficient. The machine, roughly the size of a 20-foot shipping container, can be deployed in disaster-stricken regions or arid areas, providing immediate access to safe drinking water. Growing up in a refugee community in Jordan without running water inspired Yaghi’s commitment to

item.title: Lily Allen Announces Relationship with Jonah Freud, Descendant of Sigmund Freud
item.description: Lily Allen has officially confirmed her new relationship with Jonah Freud, the great-great-grandson of the renowned psychoanalyst Sigmund Freud. This announcement comes nearly a year after her separation from actor David Harbour. The couple had previously fueled speculation after being seen together in Paris and Rome, including appearances at Paris Fashion Week. Allen mentioned the relationship casually in a recent Grazia interview, referring to Jonah as “my boyfriend” when asked about her recent messages. The singer’s new romance coincides with a significant career period, following the release of her personal album 'West End Girl,' which was written and recorded in just ten days and reflects on her past marriage breakdown. The album has received critical acclaim, ranking at No. 13 on NME’s 50 Best Albums of 2025 list, and has supported a largely sold-out tour. Notably, her song 'Pussy Palace' became a standout track of the year. Allen is now preparing for a UK theatre and arena tour,

item.title: UK-Designed Missile Strikes Russian Defense Plant Amid Ukraine Conflict
item.description: On February 25, 2026, Ukraine launched a medium-range missile targeting the Votkinsk Machine-Building Plant in Udmurtia, a key Russian defense facility involved in strategic missile production. The missile, known as the FP-5 Flamingo, caused significant damage to Workshop 19, a hub for producing ICBMs such as Topol-M, Yars, and submarine-launched Bulava missiles, injuring at least 11 workers. The attack has raised concerns about Russia's ability to maintain its strategic missile production and coincides with escalating tensions over NATO and U.S. missile deployments in Ukraine. The FP-5 Flamingo, designed by the UK with some Ukrainian components, represents a new capability for Ukraine to strike deep into Russian territory. Russian officials have viewed such deployments as a direct threat to national security, and previous warnings and failed negotiations with the U.S. and NATO have heightened the stakes. Analysts warn that the attack could be interpreted as a provocation by the West,

item.title: Russia Increases Oil Exports Despite Falling Revenues
item.description: Despite ongoing Western sanctions and efforts to block its oil trade since the beginning of the Ukraine War four years ago, Russia's crude oil exports have not diminished. According to a report by the Finnish Centre for Research on Energy and Clean Air (CREA), Russia exported 215 million tons of crude in the fourth year of the war, marking a 6% decrease in volume compared to the previous year but a 6% increase over pre-war levels. However, revenue from these exports fell 18% in one year, showing that Moscow had to sell oil at a discount. Total fossil fuel revenues reached €193 billion, down 19% from the previous year and 27% below pre-war levels, yet still substantial. China, India, and Turkey remained Russia's main buyers, accounting for 93% of crude exports, although their annual imports slightly declined due to U.S. sanctions. European buyers like Hungary and Slovakia continue to rely on Russian oil under EU exemptions, with Hungary increasing its dependence to 92%. Russia also miti

item.title: European Military Aid to Ukraine Reaches Its Limits Amid Shortages
item.description: European countries are reportedly reaching the limits of their military support to Ukraine, as key nations indicate they can no longer supply additional arms. Finnish Foreign Minister Elina Valtonen stated that Finland has already sent all available assistance to Kyiv, despite its contributions being above the European average. Germany's Defense Minister Boris Pistorius confirmed that German arsenals are exhausted, while Denmark emphasized its focus on financing drone production rather than providing more equipment. This shortage comes at a critical time for Ukraine, which faces severe ammunition shortages for its air defense systems. Meanwhile, Russian missile attacks continue to target Ukrainian energy infrastructure with minimal resistance. The European hope now rests on a €90 billion aid package, which is stalled due to Hungary’s refusal to approve it. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky has urged European partners to respond to his military’s needs, but repeated statements of d

item.title: Brandi Carlile Raises $600,000 for Families Affected by Immigration Enforcement in Minneapolis
item.description: Grammy-winning singer Brandi Carlile transformed her Minneapolis concert into a significant fundraiser, generating over $600,000 to support families impacted by recent Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) actions in the city. The event, titled 'Be Human: A Concert for Minneapolis,' was held at the Target Center on February 21 and livestreamed for broader access, drawing more than 12,000 attendees. Proceeds were donated to the Minnesota-based organization Advocates for Human Rights, which works to support and represent families affected by immigration enforcement. Carlile also raised additional funds through the sale of $40 'Be Human' T-shirts, inspired by her latest album 'Human.' The concert responded to Operation Metro Surge, a federal initiative deploying up to 3,000 ICE and Border Patrol agents to Minneapolis and Saint Paul. Reports indicate widespread detentions, including U.S. citizens, which led to public outcry and a city-wide strike. During the show, Carlile performed wit

item.title: Geometric Projection Mapping in Natural Landscapes by Javier Riera
item.description: Spanish designer Javier Riera creates striking geometric projections that interact with natural landscapes, transforming organic environments into visual experiments in shape and light. Using custom-mapped projections, Riera overlays rigid geometric forms onto forests, deserts, and other outdoor settings, creating a dialogue between structured design and natural randomness. This interplay highlights contrasts between human-made order and the organic irregularity of nature, inviting viewers to reconsider their perceptions of space and form. His work is not only visually arresting but also conceptually engaging, as it explores the relationship between architecture, art, and the environment. Riera's approach combines precision with creativity, resulting in images that appear both surreal and harmonious. By projecting geometric patterns onto trees, rocks, and landscapes, he creates temporary installations that merge technology with the natural world, emphasizing the potential of projection

item.title: Essential Maintenance Tips to Preserve Your Property’s Appearance
item.description: Maintaining the visual appeal of a property requires proactive care and understanding the factors that cause damage over time. Environmental elements like pollutants, mineral deposits, algae, and UV rays gradually deteriorate surfaces, often unnoticed until damage becomes visible. Regular cleaning restores the original colors of exterior walls, concrete, and stucco, with methods like hot water pressure washing effectively removing deep-set grime. Timing is crucial: spring cleaning addresses winter salt and mold, while fall cleaning prepares surfaces for freezing temperatures. Different materials need tailored approaches; wood, stone, and concrete require specific pressures and chemicals to avoid permanent damage. Windows benefit from professional cleaning to remove hard water spots, rust, pollen, and city pollution, improving sunlight transmission and even the efficiency of solar panels. Protective coatings such as sealants, epoxy, and specialized metal treatments prevent moisture, sun

item.title: How Age-Verification Laws Are Expanding Digital Surveillance and Undermining Privacy
item.description:
The article examines the growing push for mandatory age-verification systems on social media platforms and argues that these measures create a structural conflict with data-privacy principles. As lawmakers seek to protect minors from harmful online content, they increasingly require platforms to take “reasonable” or “effective” steps to prevent underage access. However, the author contends that meaningful enforcement inevitably requires collecting and retaining personal data, which contradicts modern privacy standards that emphasize data minimization and limited retention.
Platforms typically rely on two main approaches: identity-based verification, such as uploading government IDs, and inference-based systems, including facial age estimation and behavioral analysis. In practice, companies combine both methods. Self-declared ages are monitored by AI systems, and when uncertainty arises, users may be asked for selfies, official documents, or even credit card verification. These process

item.title: War Losses, Falling Birthrates and Migration Deepen Ukraine’s Demographic Crisis
item.description:
As the war with Russia approaches its fourth year, Ukraine is facing a severe demographic crisis marked by heavy military casualties, plummeting birth rates, and mass migration. According to leading demographer Ella Libanova, the country has lost around 10 million people since the conflict began, due to deaths, displacement, and occupation. Fertility rates have dropped below one child per woman, significantly lower than the European and US averages, worsening a decline that had already begun before the war.
The human toll of the conflict is visible in stories like that of Olena Bilozerska, a former soldier who delayed motherhood to serve on the frontlines. After struggling with fertility issues and undergoing in vitro fertilization, her embryo remained frozen in Kyiv as Russia launched its full-scale invasion. She eventually gave birth at 46, but her experience reflects the difficult choices many Ukrainian women face between military service and family life.
Medical professionals at

item.title: Mexico Urged to Develop a National AI Strategy to Strengthen Economic Growth
item.description: Centro México Digital has released a comprehensive study titled “Artificial Intelligence in Mexico: From Promise to Economic Impact,” analyzing the effects of AI adoption on Mexican businesses using data from the 2024 Economic Census by INEGI, covering 5.2 million companies. The study highlights that AI adoption is a critical driver of economic performance, showing that a 10% increase in AI adoption correlates with a 5.2% increase in gross production and a 3.8% rise in average wages. However, the impact on employment varies across companies, aligning with international research on AI’s labor effects. Compared internationally, Mexico remains in the early stages of AI adoption, with only 8% of companies with ten or more employees using AI, compared to an OECD average of 20.2%. Large Mexican companies adopt AI at 17%, while small businesses lag at 6%, revealing a 2.8x adoption gap. Geographic disparities are also evident, with states like Nuevo León, Tamaulipas, and Mexico City leading ad

item.title: From Gutenberg’s Printing Press to Generative AI: The Dialectics of Knowledge and the Revival of Orality
item.description:
The article reflects on the historical transformation of knowledge transmission, drawing a parallel between the invention of Gutenberg’s movable-type printing press in the 15th century and the current rise of generative artificial intelligence. In medieval Europe and many earlier cultures, knowledge was primarily oral, conveyed through sermons, troubadours, university debates, and monastic readings. Memory and rhetoric were central intellectual skills, and learning was inseparable from communal listening and recitation.
With the arrival of the printing press, knowledge became more accessible and affordable, but this technological shift also generated anxiety. Some humanists feared that mass-produced books would weaken memory, encourage intellectual laziness, and flood society with low-quality texts. Printed knowledge separated information from the authority of the individual teacher and transformed it into a commodity. Despite these concerns, the printing revolution did not destroy or

item.title: Deregulation of Electricity in Ohio Increased Costs Through Middlemen
item.description: Recent research from The Ohio State University’s Energy Markets and Policy Group shows that electricity deregulation in several U.S. states, including Ohio, has led to higher, not lower, prices for consumers. Between the late 1990s and early 2000s, deregulation split electricity services, allowing wholesale generation markets to compete, while transmission and distribution remained regulated. Retail consumers could choose to buy electricity through open-market suppliers or stick with default service, but most people chose the latter. Researchers found that 72% of open-market offers were more expensive than default rates, indicating that competition did not deliver the promised savings. Suppliers often set their prices based on the default service auction outcomes rather than market fundamentals. Additionally, default service auctions were found to have limited competition, and fewer bidders resulted in higher electricity rates. Adding just a few extra bidders could significantly lower

item.title: Dutch Prime Minister-designate Rob Jetten reaffirms past criticisms of Donald Trump ahead of inauguration
item.description: Rob Jetten, leader of the Dutch liberal party D66 and the incoming Prime Minister of the Netherlands set to be sworn in shortly after February 21, 2026, has confirmed he stands by his previous strong criticisms of U.S. President Donald Trump. In past statements, particularly around Trump's 2024 re-election, Jetten described Trump as a 'convicted criminal' referring to U.S. court rulings, a 'misogynist,' someone who 'openly flirts with dictators,' and a 'danger to international security and the global economy.' He refused to congratulate Trump on his victory at the time. In a recent interview with RTL Nieuws, Jetten said he would not retract these remarks, arguing that true friends can be critical of each other when values are at stake. He noted that diplomatic discussions would occur upon meeting Trump in person, though a more measured tone might be necessary in his new role. The article highlights a broader shift in European views toward the U.S. under Trump, with growing doubts about

item.title: USS Gerald R. Ford Faces Persistent Sewage System Failures During Extended Deployment Amid U.S.-Iran Tensions
item.description: The USS Gerald R. Ford, the U.S. Navy's most advanced and expensive aircraft carrier at $13.3 billion, is grappling with chronic sewage system problems while deployed in the Middle East. The nuclear-powered vessel, home to over 4,500 sailors, has been at sea since June 2025—now in its eighth month, far exceeding the standard six-month deployment period. This extension, driven by escalating tensions with Iran following President Donald Trump's threats and potential strike preparations, has compounded crew fatigue and mental strain. Many sailors, mostly in their early 20s, report missing significant family events like birthdays, weddings, births, or funerals, leading some to consider leaving the military. The core issue lies in the ship's vacuum-based sewage system, which features narrow pipes prone to calcium buildup and clogs. A single valve failure can disable toilets across entire sections, resulting in most of the 650 toilets being out of service at times. Sailors face daily queues

item.title: Discord ends partnership with Persona after exposed frontend code raises surveillance and data handling concerns
item.description:
Discord has ended a brief partnership with Persona, an identity verification company backed in part by Peter Thiel’s Founders Fund, after researchers discovered that Persona’s frontend code was publicly accessible and hosted on a U.S. government-authorized endpoint. The exposed files, totaling roughly 53 megabytes, revealed that Persona’s systems are capable of conducting extensive verification checks, including facial recognition against watchlists, screening for politically exposed persons, and scanning for adverse media across categories such as terrorism and espionage. Researchers claimed they did not need to exploit vulnerabilities, as the architecture was openly accessible.
Persona CEO Rick Song disputed claims that the exposure constituted a serious security flaw, describing the files as uncompressed frontend code similar to what is already delivered to users’ devices. He emphasized that while the contents may appear alarming, they did not represent a major vulnerability. Song

item.title: Remarkable Trees Across the Globe and Their Unique Features
item.description: Trees are some of the most extraordinary living organisms on Earth, offering not only beauty but also a glimpse into history and survival. This article highlights several remarkable trees from around the world, each with unique characteristics and astonishing longevity. The baobab, often called the 'Tree of Life,' can store up to 120,000 liters of water in its enormous trunk and is found across Africa, Madagascar, and Australia. Mexico's ahuehuete cypress trees have thrived for over 1,400 years, witnessing generations rise and fall beneath their vast canopies. Northern California's coast redwoods are the tallest living trees on Earth, reaching heights over 380 feet and creating awe-inspiring forest environments. Other visually stunning species include dragon blood trees on Socotra Island, Japan's 144-year-old wisteria, and a 1,400-year-old gingko tree in China's Zhongnan Mountains, which showers golden leaves every autumn. These examples illustrate the planet's remarkable tree diversit

item.title: Russell Brand Denies New Rape and Sexual Assault Allegations in London Court
item.description: British comedian and actor Russell Brand, 50, appeared at Southwark Crown Court in London on February 24, 2026, to plead not guilty to two new charges of rape and sexual assault. The alleged incidents reportedly occurred in 2009, involving two women. This development follows earlier charges filed last year, in which Brand faced two counts of rape, one count of indecent assault, and two counts of sexual assault against four women between 1999 and 2005. Brand had previously pleaded not guilty to those charges in May 2023, with the trial scheduled for June 2024. A court hearing next month will determine whether the new accusations will be included in the same trial. Brand’s lawyer has requested additional time to respond to the latest allegations. Known for his flamboyant style, outspoken personality, and past fame as a radio presenter for the BBC, television host, and actor in films such as Get Him to the Greek (2010), Brand arrived at court wearing a white cowboy hat and sunglasses. Whe

item.title: Rising Sigilkore Rapper Luci4 Dies at 23 in Los Angeles
item.description:
Rapper Luci4, born James Dear and also known by the stage name Axxturel, has died at the age of 23. According to a report from the Los Angeles County Medical Examiner, he passed away on Sunday, February 22, at a private residence in Los Angeles. His grandparents told TMZ that he died at a friend’s house, and authorities reportedly found his wallet empty when they arrived at the scene. The Los Angeles Fire Department pronounced him dead at 11:40 a.m., and the Los Angeles Police Department has launched an investigation into the circumstances surrounding his death.
Luci4 was considered a rising talent in the emerging microgenre known as sigilkore, a substyle of trap music that blends electronic elements with cloud rap influences. He first gained significant attention in 2021 through TikTok, where his music quickly resonated with a young audience. His growing popularity led to a record deal with Atlantic Records.
His breakout single, “BodyPartz,” achieved major commercial success and was

item.title: Foreign Patients Increasingly Visit Chinese Hospitals for Affordable and Efficient Medical Care
item.description: In recent years, China has become a popular destination for international medical tourists seeking affordable and fast healthcare. Cities like Shenzhen and Shanghai have seen significant increases in foreign patient visits. In 2024, Shenzhen recorded 770,000 visits from overseas patients, while 13 top hospitals in Shanghai reported a 15 percent rise, totaling nearly 270,000 foreign patients. Social media platforms such as TikTok highlight this trend, with many foreigners sharing their experiences of cost-effective and efficient medical care in China. One major factor attracting visitors is the dramatic price difference: a heart checkup that could cost up to $20,000 in the US costs around $75 in China, and simple X-rays can be cheaper than toy doctor kits in Western stores. British influencer Amy, for example, received a diagnosis for a two-year undiagnosed stomach issue on the first day of her visit. Popular medical services among tourists include dentistry, optometry, and Traditional

item.title: US Women’s Olympic Hockey Team Declines State of the Union Invitation Due to Schedule Conflicts
item.description: The gold medal-winning U.S. women’s Olympic hockey team has declined an invitation to attend President Donald Trump’s State of the Union address, citing prior scheduling conflicts with academic and professional commitments following the Olympics. The invitation came after Trump celebrated the men’s team’s Olympic victory by phone, even offering a military plane for their travel to Washington, D.C., and mentioning that the women’s team should attend as well. Members of the women’s team were formally invited, but they issued a statement expressing gratitude for the recognition while noting they could not participate due to their commitments. The men’s team, which also won gold against Canada, has not confirmed whether they will attend. Social media reacted strongly to the interaction between Trump and the women’s team during the call. The U.S. women’s hockey team, who also won gold in PyeongChang during Trump’s first term, now return to the Professional Women’s Hockey League, while the m

item.title: Matty Healy and Gabbriette Bechtel reportedly plan July wedding in California
item.description: According to a report by The Sun, Matty Healy, lead singer of The 1975, is reportedly set to marry model and singer Gabbriette Bechtel in July 2026 in California. This potential wedding would occur just weeks after the alleged wedding date of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce, which The Sun states is scheduled for June 13 in Watch Hill, Rhode Island, although neither Swift nor Kelce has publicly confirmed any wedding plans. Healy and Swift were briefly linked in 2023, with their relationship reportedly lasting about two months, sparking fan speculation and discussions, especially after the release of Swift’s album 'The Tortured Poets Department'. Following the split with Swift, Healy began dating Bechtel in 2023. A friend told The Sun that the couple aims for a lavish celebration, with no expense spared, emphasizing their excitement and party spirit. If the reported dates are accurate, both Swift and Healy could be having ceremonies within weeks of one another this summer. However, as neit

item.title: Dakota Johnson reflects on her past relationship with Chris Martin and moves forward
item.description: Dakota Johnson has reportedly moved on from her on-and-off relationship with Coldplay singer Chris Martin, which lasted for eight years and ended last year. According to sources, Johnson regrets giving Martin multiple chances during their relationship and feels she stayed longer than she should have after their connection turned toxic. Friends of the actor had expressed concerns that she was still vulnerable to Martin, but Johnson has reportedly made it clear she is ready to move forward. Recently, Johnson has been linked to musician Tucker Pillsbury, known professionally as Role Model. Sources say she is enjoying her time with Pillsbury and that the relationship has helped her gain perspective on what she had been missing while still involved with Martin. Though she reportedly still cares about her former partner, Johnson has made it clear that she does not intend to rekindle their romance. The new relationship appears to be giving her the support and distraction she needed to fully m

item.title: Practical Strategies for Combining Comfort and Style in Home Design
item.description: This article explores how to harmonize aesthetics and functionality in home design by prioritizing comfort alongside visual appeal. It emphasizes the importance of understanding how people experience a space throughout the day, noting temperature swings, airflow, and odors that photos cannot capture. Mapping comfort issues, such as drafts, hot or cold spots, and pressure imbalances, is recommended before choosing furniture or layouts. Proper placement and design of HVAC components, including vents, returns, and thermostats, are essential to maintain both performance and appearance. The piece also highlights material choices that respond to humidity, protect finishes, and integrate biophilic design elements to create healthier, more resilient spaces. Zoning, smart controls, and attention to air circulation ensure that rooms are comfortable without constant manual adjustments. Regular maintenance, including filter changes and duct sealing, prevents system inefficiencies that can disrupt

item.title: Computational Study Finds 40,000-Year-Old Stone Age Sign Sequences Comparable in Complexity to Proto-Cuneiform
item.description:
A new study by linguist Christian Bentz of Saarland University and archaeologist Ewa Dutkiewicz from the Museum of Prehistory and Early History in Berlin suggests that sign sequences carved into Stone Age objects over 40,000 years ago may represent an early system of visual information encoding. The research, soon to be published in the journal PNAS, analyzed more than 3,000 geometric signs found on around 260 Paleolithic artefacts dating from 34,000 to 45,000 years ago.
Many of these objects were discovered in caves in the Swabian Jura region of southwestern Germany, including the Vogelherd Cave, Geißenklösterle Cave, and Hohlenstein-Stadel Cave. Artefacts such as a mammoth figurine carved from ivory and the so-called “Adorant” plate depicting a lion-human hybrid feature repeated patterns of crosses, dots, lines, and notches. The researchers used computational methods, statistical modeling, and machine learning to examine the frequency, repetition, and predictability of these signs.

item.title: Drone Warfare and Electronic Networks Reshape the Front Lines in Ukraine
item.description:
The article describes how the war in Ukraine has transformed into a highly technological and electronically driven conflict, where drones and communication networks dominate the battlefield. The front line is portrayed as a 1,200-kilometer stretch with a 'grey zone' between 20 and 40 kilometers wide. Alongside traditional trenches, the area is now covered with fishing nets and extensive fiber optic cables, reflecting the growing importance of counter-drone measures and secure communications.
According to the report, modern warfare in Ukraine increasingly depends on the quality of the connection between drone operators and their devices. Aerial, naval, and ground drones are central to combat operations, and both sides are engaged in constant innovation to improve performance and autonomy. The article emphasizes the rapid pace of technological change, noting that artificial intelligence is becoming essential to guide and target drones more efficiently, reducing reliance on human operato

item.title: Global Report Highlights Concentration of Geopolitical Cyberattacks in Ten Key Countries
item.description:
A recent annual Global Cyber Threat Report published by Radware indicates that cyberattacks have become a central indirect instrument in modern geopolitical conflicts. According to the report, 80 percent of politically motivated cyberattacks are concentrated in just ten countries, demonstrating a high degree of geographic focus. Israel tops the list with 1,881 claimed attacks in the past year, accounting for 12.2 percent of the global total. The surge is largely linked to ongoing tensions involving Hamas, Iran, and aligned groups, with pro-Iranian cells reportedly claiming responsibility for many of the incidents.
The United States and Ukraine follow, with 1,448 and 1,373 attacks respectively, reflecting their exposure to strategic rivalries in the digital domain. Germany and France also appear prominently, with 849 and 840 attacks, separated by only a narrow margin. Other countries in the top ten include Thailand, Italy, Spain, India, and Poland, illustrating the global scope of cybe

item.title: Russia Links Attempted Assassination of General Alexeiev to International Involvement
item.description: On February 6, 2026, an attempt was made on the life of Russian General Vladimir Alexeiev, who was shot multiple times but survived after being hospitalized and undergoing surgery. Russian authorities, including the Federal Security Service (FSB), quickly identified the shooter, Lioubomir Korba, who was later apprehended in Dubai and extradited to Russia. His accomplices, Viktor Vasin and Zinaida Serebritskaya, were also tracked, with Vasin detained in Moscow and Serebritskaya fleeing to Turkey under an international arrest warrant. Alexander Bortnikov, FSB Director, stated that the attack was sponsored by the Ukrainian intelligence services but included the participation of other countries, notably the United Kingdom, and alleged that Kyiv acted under the guidance of Western intelligence. Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov condemned the attempt as an act of terrorism, suggesting it aimed to disrupt peace negotiations in Ukraine. The FSB continues to investigate and promised to rel

item.title: The Benefits of Buying Windows and Doors from Authorized Dealers
item.description:
The article explains why selecting an authorized dealer for windows and doors is a critical decision for homeowners seeking quality, durability, and long-term value. While many buyers focus on style, materials, or pricing, the source of purchase plays an equally important role. Authorized dealers guarantee genuine products that meet manufacturer standards, protecting customers from counterfeit or substandard goods. This ensures that materials, finishes, and performance features align with brand promises.
Another key advantage highlighted is expert knowledge and professional support. Authorized dealers employ trained staff who understand product specifications, installation requirements, and maintenance guidelines. Their expertise helps customers choose the most suitable options for their projects while avoiding costly mistakes. In addition, many authorized dealers oversee the installation process, ensuring proper fitting and optimal performance.
Warranty protection is another major b

item.title: A Geological Exploration of Eight Remarkable Gemstones and Their Natural Origins
item.description:
The article explores eight extraordinary gemstones formed through natural geological processes over millions or even billions of years. It emphasizes that their beauty is not intentional, but rather the result of chemistry, pressure, heat, and time acting deep within the Earth’s crust. Labradorite from Canada is highlighted for its striking labradorescence, an optical phenomenon caused by light interacting with microscopic feldspar layers, producing shifting hues reminiscent of the aurora borealis. Myanmar’s rubies, especially those from the Mogok Valley, are prized for their intense “pigeon’s blood” red coloration, created by precise concentrations of chromium. Sri Lanka’s sapphires, formed in ancient metamorphic rock dating back to the Gondwana supercontinent, are renowned for their luminous cornflower-blue tone and clarity.
The article also examines malachite from the Democratic Republic of Congo, whose layered green bands form through copper-rich water deposits over long periods.

item.title: Mercedes-Benz Launches a Master-Planned Residential Community in Dubai
item.description: Mercedes-Benz, in collaboration with luxury developer Binghatti, is creating an ambitious new project in Dubai called Mercedes-Benz Places Binghatti City. Envisioned as a 'city within a city,' the development will cover nearly 9 million square feet and feature over 13,000 residences. The project aims to combine residential living with lifestyle and luxury amenities, including green spaces, pools, fitness facilities, sports areas, event halls, a ballroom, and a private screening lounge. The design concept heavily reflects Mercedes-Benz's aesthetic, with interiors featuring black and silver tones complemented by leather and wood accents for warmth. Architecturally, the development consists of 12 towers following a unified design language, each named after Mercedes concept cars such as Vision One-Eleven, Vision AVTR, and Vision Mercedes-Maybach 6, while a central tower is designated as Vision Iconic. Binghatti positions the development as more than branded residences, intending it to func

item.title: Investigation Alleges Israeli Forces Shot and Killed 15 Gaza Aid Workers During March 2025 Operation
item.description:
A joint investigation by the research groups Earshot and Forensic Architecture alleges that Israeli soldiers killed 15 Palestinian aid workers in Tel al-Sultan, southern Gaza, on March 23, 2025. According to the report, Israeli forces fired at least 910 shots over the course of more than two hours, with the majority discharged within a five-and-a-half-minute period. The victims included eight members of the Palestine Red Crescent Society, six Civil Defense workers, and one UN relief staff member. The findings are based on survivor testimony, audio and video recordings, satellite imagery, and digital reconstructions of the घटनts.
The investigation concludes that soldiers positioned on an elevated sandbank opened fire on clearly marked emergency vehicles with active lights, later advancing toward the vehicles while continuing to shoot. Audio analysis using echolocation techniques suggests that at least some victims were shot at close range, with several bullets fired from as little as o

item.title: Anonymous 15-Year-Old Girl Describes Daily Exposure to Misogynistic Abuse and Objectification on Social Media
item.description: In this anonymous opinion piece, a 15-year-old British schoolgirl details the pervasive and inescapable misogyny she encounters daily on mainstream platforms like Instagram and TikTok. Despite actively avoiding such content, she is bombarded with degrading language and attitudes: terms like 'females,' 'thots,' 'community pussy,' 'bops,' and 'foid' dehumanize girls and women, while comments often objectify bodies, trivialize rape, mock victims, rate attractiveness harshly, and promote double standards on sexual experience—praising boys for promiscuity but shaming girls as 'used up' or worthless based on 'body count.' She shares examples, including vile responses to a rape survivor's post and unprompted hate in girls' comment sections. This exposure severely impacts her self-esteem, leading to body hatred, discomfort with her gender, resentment toward boys, doubts about future relationships, and a sense of generational gender divide. She acknowledges boys are also influenced by harmful a

item.title: A Unified Geopolitical Conflict Linking Iran, Ukraine, and Venezuela
item.description:
The article argues that the conflicts surrounding Iran, Ukraine, and Venezuela are not isolated crises but rather interconnected fronts of a broader geopolitical struggle. According to the author, this overarching conflict seeks to prevent the decline of American-Western global hegemony, which is increasingly challenged by China’s rise. In Ukraine, the objective is described as weakening Russia, considered China’s key strategic partner. In Venezuela, the aim is portrayed as limiting China’s access to major energy reserves and Latin American resources. Iran is presented as a central link in Eurasian integration, connecting energy and transport corridors across East-West and North-South axes.
The piece claims that the United States is preparing a significant military operation against Iran, noting naval deployments and diplomatic efforts to secure regional backing. It highlights concerns among Gulf states about the risk of a broader regional war and suggests that Iran’s potential retali

item.title: DOJ Faces Questions After Epstein-Related Record Briefly Disappears From Public Database
item.description:
The article examines controversy surrounding the U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) after a record connected to the Jeffrey Epstein case briefly disappeared from a public database before being restored. The record reportedly references FBI interviews with a woman who accused former President Donald Trump of sexual assault as a child. According to the report, the FBI conducted four interviews with the accuser in 2019, but only one of those interviews was included in the publicly released Epstein-related materials.
The temporary removal of the database entry raised concerns about transparency, particularly because Ghislaine Maxwell’s legal team may have received access to all four interviews as part of trial discovery. Critics argue that if Maxwell’s defense had access to more complete materials than the public, it creates an imbalance in information and fuels speculation about selective disclosure. The article stops short of providing evidence that documents were destroyed, but frames th

item.title: Alleged Use of Starlink Terminals in Iran and U.S. Covert Operations
item.description: A recent article claims that the United States supplied thousands of Starlink satellite terminals to Iran with the alleged goal of circumventing censorship and supporting anti-government actors. According to the report, these terminals were introduced clandestinely and were intended to provide communication capabilities to groups opposing the Iranian government. The article cites supposed involvement of the U.S. Department of State and SpaceX in coordinating the distribution, suggesting that the operation was linked to former President Donald Trump's administration. It mentions prior U.S. efforts to support internet access in Iran through VPN providers, which faced limitations when internet was blocked. The report also highlights that Iranian authorities were able to counteract the Starlink systems by manipulating GPS signals, rendering some terminals inoperative. While the piece frames these actions as part of a U.S. strategy to destabilize Iran, it should be noted that the claims rel

item.title: Reports Highlight Sanitation Problems Aboard USS Gerald R. Ford Amid Broader Military Operations
item.description:
The article reports on alleged sanitation system failures aboard the USS Gerald R. Ford, the lead ship of its class and one of the most expensive aircraft carriers ever built by the United States Navy. According to the piece, U.S. public broadcaster NPR obtained internal emails describing persistent malfunctions in the ship’s vacuum-based toilet system. The Wall Street Journal is cited as confirming the existence of these issues. With a crew of approximately 4,600 sailors, the vessel reportedly has too few operational toilets, leading to long lines and repeated breakdowns.
The article claims that design flaws and a fragile vacuum system have contributed to the situation, where the failure of a single valve can disable toilets across an entire department. It states that over a four-day period in March 2025, more than 200 toilet malfunctions were recorded. Temporary repairs, such as chemical flushes to remove calcium buildup, allegedly can only be performed while docked, limiting the cr

item.title: 50 Cent Continues Social Media Feud with T.I. by Targeting King and Tiny Harris
item.description:
Rapper 50 Cent has once again ignited controversy on social media after posting comments aimed at King Harris, the son of fellow rapper T.I., and his mother, Tameka “Tiny” Harris. The incident stems from remarks T.I. made during an appearance on the podcast Million Dollars Worth of Game, where he challenged 50 Cent to a one-on-one confrontation and stated that he had lost respect for him. In response, 50 Cent shared an unflattering image of Tiny Harris on social media, adding a pointed caption telling others to keep his name out of their mouths.
This episode reflects what many fans describe as a recurring pattern in 50 Cent’s public feuds: escalating disputes by involving family members of his rivals. The article references a similar situation from the previous year involving Memphis rapper Finesse2Times. In that instance, 50 Cent mocked the rapper online over allegations that he was not financially supporting his mother, posting sarcastic and critical comments that quickly drew backl

item.title: Exploring the Emotional Depth of AI-Generated Vaporwave Portraits
item.description: Byone Lab is an innovative AI art project that transforms imperfections in reality into captivating visual experiences. Its videos simulate the feel of broadcast signals capturing unintended moments, creating a unique aesthetic where glitches, neon distortions, and encoding artifacts evoke both emotion and vitality. Unlike typical vaporwave, these works are less about design and more about discovery, treated with the patience of a nature documentarian. A standout feature of the project is the use of GPS tagging, giving context and meaning to the recurring glitches, which feel deliberately placed rather than random. The combination of technological experimentation and artistic expression results in visuals that invite viewers to revisit and explore, highlighting the intersection of AI, art, and the unexpected beauty found in imperfection. Byone Lab’s approach challenges traditional digital art norms by turning what would usually be considered errors into focal points that enrich the sto

item.title: London Startup Develops Biodegradable Leather Alternative from Brewery Waste Grain
item.description:
A London-based startup, Arda Biomaterials, is transforming spent grain from breweries and distilleries into a sustainable leather alternative called New Grain™. The innovation addresses two major environmental concerns associated with traditional leather production and synthetic substitutes. Conventional leather relies on animal hides, which require significant land, water, and feed resources, while many vegan leather options depend on petroleum-based plastics that contribute to pollution and microplastic shedding. Arda’s solution repurposes one of the brewing industry’s most abundant byproducts: protein-rich grain left over after beer production.
Using principles of green chemistry, the company restructures plant proteins into strong, fibrous sheets that replicate the look, feel, and performance of leather. The material can be adjusted in terms of softness, thickness, texture, and color, making it suitable for fashion and design applications. Early collaborations have included vegan

item.title: Why Total Screen Luminance Matters More Than Blue Light Filters for Sleep
item.description:
In this article, visual neuroscientist Patrick Mineault argues that blue light filters, such as Apple’s Night Shift or apps like f.lux, are largely ineffective at improving sleep. While the common belief is that reducing blue light exposure at night helps regulate circadian rhythms, Mineault explains that this idea oversimplifies how the brain’s biological clock functions. The suprachiasmatic nucleus (SCN), the brain’s master clock, is influenced by intrinsically photosensitive retinal ganglion cells (ipRGCs), which respond to overall light levels rather than exclusively to blue wavelengths. Although melanopsin—the light-sensitive molecule in these cells—has peak sensitivity in the blue-cyan range, it also responds broadly across blue and green wavelengths. Simply shifting screen colors away from blue only reduces relevant light input by about 50%, which is minor on the logarithmic scale of human light perception.
Using measurements from a colorimeter on a MacBook Air, Mineault found

item.title: Secret Service fatally shoots intruder at Mar-a-Lago; suspect linked to Epstein files
item.description: A 21-year-old man, Austin Tucker Martin, was shot and killed by Secret Service agents after attempting to break into former President Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago residence in Florida early Sunday morning. Martin, from Moore County, North Carolina, entered the resort's north gate armed with a shotgun and a gas canister. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump were not at the residence at the time, being in Washington, D.C. for an official dinner. Martin's family, described as 'avid Trump supporters,' reported him missing the previous day. His cousin described him as quiet, charitable, and inexperienced with firearms. Palm Beach County Sheriff Ric Bradshaw stated that agents confronted Martin and ordered him to drop his weapons, but he raised the shotgun, prompting officers to shoot. No law enforcement personnel were injured. The FBI is leading the investigation into the incident. White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt commended the Secret Service's quick response. Attorney General Pam Bo

item.title: Gisele Bündchen Seen Wearing Wedding Ring During Bike Ride Amid Ongoing Attention on Tom Brady’s Dating Life
item.description:
Gisele Bündchen was recently spotted enjoying a quiet bike ride with her youngest son in Florida, drawing attention as she appeared relaxed and unbothered amid renewed public interest in her ex-husband Tom Brady’s rumored dating life. The supermodel was photographed riding along a hedge-lined street dressed casually in beige activewear and a baseball cap, with her child seated securely in a front-mounted bike seat. Observers noted that she was wearing a wedding ring, a visible symbol of her recent marriage to partner Joaquim Valente.
Bündchen and Valente reportedly held a private wedding ceremony in December at their home in Surfside, Florida. The event was described as intimate and low-key. The public sighting of her wedding ring comes at a time when Brady has been linked romantically to TikTok personality Alix Earle. The two were recently seen together in St. Barths, prompting media speculation about the nature of their relationship. Reports have suggested they are romantically invo

item.title: Allegations of Abuse and Deaths at Fort Bliss Migrant Detention Center
item.description: A group of Texas legislators has raised serious concerns over alleged torture, killings, and inhumane treatment of migrants at the Fort Bliss detention center in El Paso, the largest U.S. facility of its kind. Reports indicate that three detainees have died within two months, including a Cuban migrant reportedly strangled by guards. The center, part of the Trump administration’s efforts to accelerate deportations, was built in August and can hold up to 5,000 people under a $1.2 billion private contract. Inspections by ICE revealed over 60 violations of internal detention standards in the first 50 days, including lack of access to proper sanitation, food, and communication with family or legal counsel. Congresswoman Verónica Escobar reported cases of tuberculosis and COVID-19 among detainees. Human rights groups have also highlighted unlawful deportation attempts and threats against detainees. Allegations include severe physical and sexual abuse by guards. Lawmakers are demanding a thor

item.title: Primal Fathers, Billionaire Households, and the Political Economy of Sexual Power
item.description:
This article examines the ideological and economic logic linking Silicon Valley pronatalism, far-right political movements, and the household empire built by Jeffrey Epstein. It begins by describing the rise of “primal fathers” among segments of the American far right—wealthy, hyper-individualist men who seek not merely large families but dynastic genetic legacies. Figures like Elon Musk are presented as emblematic of a transhumanist vision that exalts exceptionalism rather than merely eliminating perceived genetic “defects.” Drawing on Freud’s concept of the primal horde from *Totem and Taboo*, the author argues that this mythology of the dominant patriarch and rival brothers has reemerged in modern tech culture and founder-controlled firms.
The essay then turns to Jeffrey Epstein as a case study of how patriarchal and “fratriarchal” systems of sexual control can merge. Epstein’s household operated as a hybrid economy in which women and girls were treated either as exclusive property

item.title: Updated Analysis of Global Industrial and Population Trends
item.description: This article examines recent updates to the World3 model, originally developed in the 1970s to study the interaction between population growth, industrial production, resource consumption, and environmental impacts. Berndt Warm recalibrated the model using current global data, with a focus on Germany and motor vehicle production as proxies for broader industrial activity. His findings suggest that global industrial output may have peaked, with a potential decline in population starting around 2030. Regional variations are evident: Europe shows signs of early decline due to limited domestic energy resources and industrial challenges, while China maintains strong growth, particularly in coal production. Warm identifies that previous model predictions failed due to incomplete data and insufficient consideration of renewable energy, infrastructure longevity, and recent economic disruptions such as COVID-19. Updated simulations indicate that global fossil fuel extraction is approaching its

item.title: German Steel Companies Suspend Energy Transition Projects Amid Economic Crisis
item.description: Since last year, major German steel companies such as ArcelorMittal, Salzgitter, and Thyssenkrupp have postponed or halted their energy transition and CO2 reduction initiatives due to economic pressures. Rising energy costs, reduced domestic demand, and U.S. tariffs on steel exports have placed significant strain on the industry, threatening around 80,000 jobs. For instance, ArcelorMittal’s plan to make its Hamburg plant carbon-neutral was canceled after management assessed the financial risks. Meanwhile, Feralpi in Riesa continues to operate largely with recycled scrap metal using electric arc furnaces, which produce less CO2, but the high electricity prices still make production unprofitable. The fundamental challenge lies in the cost of energy: while technologies exist for low-carbon steel production, market prices for steel and electricity make these projects economically unsustainable. The German government has introduced subsidies to partially offset electricity costs until 2028,

item.title: Average U.S. Families Struggle to Afford Childcare, Study Shows
item.description: A recent LendingTree study highlights the growing unaffordability of childcare in the United States. Federal guidelines consider childcare affordable if it consumes no more than 7% of household income. However, with the average annual cost for caring for an infant and a four-year-old totaling $28,190, a two-child household would need an income of approximately $402,708 to meet this benchmark. The average U.S. two-child household earns just $145,656, meaning families would need a 176% income increase to afford childcare comfortably. State-level disparities are severe: in Hawaii, Nebraska, and Montana, families need more than 2.5 times the average income. Racial disparities are also evident, with American Indian and Black families requiring over 300% of current incomes to meet the affordability standard. Experts warn that these high costs contribute to declining birth rates, which fell below 1.6 children per woman in 2024. LendingTree recommends strategies such as maximizing employer ben

item.title: Ramaphosa Confirms South Africa’s Commitment to Strategic Non-Alignment Amid International Pressures
item.description:
On February 23, 2026, South African President Cyril Ramaphosa publicly reaffirmed his country’s commitment to a policy of non-alignment during the Armed Forces Day parade. He stated that South Africa would continue to pursue strategic autonomy and would not be drawn into the spheres of influence of rival global powers. His remarks come at a time of heightened geopolitical tensions and what he described as increasing external pressure, particularly from the United States.
South Africa, a member of the BRICS bloc since 2010 and the only African country in the G20, holds significant economic and political influence on the continent. According to the article, this strategic position has contributed to tensions with Washington. Former U.S. President Donald Trump is cited as criticizing South Africa’s participation in the G20 and reportedly did not attend the group’s summit in Johannesburg.
The article notes that China, Russia, and Turkey have expanded their commercial and diplomatic prese

item.title: Artemis II Launch Postponed to April Due to Helium System Issue
item.description: The launch of Artemis II, NASA’s second mission in the Artemis program, has been postponed to at least April 2026 due to an issue with the helium pressurization system on the rocket's ICPS second stage. Initially delayed because of hydrogen leaks during the first Wet Dress Rehearsal on February 2, 2026, the SLS rocket successfully completed a second propellant loading test on February 19 with no significant hydrogen leaks. Although NASA had announced a March 6 launch, the mission was delayed after a routine check detected a disruption in helium flow, which is essential for operating the RL-10B2 engine and purging fuel lines to prevent potential hazards. The problem mirrors previous issues experienced during Artemis I, highlighting the recurring technical challenges of the program. To inspect and address the helium system, the SLS must be moved back from Launch Pad 39B to the Vehicle Assembly Building (VAB), a complex and time-consuming procedure. Unlike Apollo-era Saturn V or shuttle l

item.title: Massive Exposure of One Billion Identity Records in Unsecured Vendor Database
item.description: A major data exposure was recently discovered involving a database containing roughly one billion sensitive identity records from at least 26 countries. The unsecured repository was linked to IDMerit, a global digital identity verification company used by businesses across financial services, fintech, telecommunications, insurance, and other regulated sectors. The exposed data included full names, dates of birth, physical addresses, phone numbers, email addresses, and national identification numbers, as well as identity verification logs and related metadata. Researchers found the database online without password protection or encryption, meaning anyone could have accessed or downloaded its contents. While there is no evidence that banks or other client institutions were directly breached, the exposure creates downstream risks such as identity theft, account takeover attempts, and targeted phishing attacks. The U.S., Mexico, the Philippines, Germany, and Italy were among the countries

item.title: Media Manipulation in the Ukraine War: Glenn Diesen at the UN Security Council
item.description: This video features Glenn Diesen, a researcher, presenting at the UN Security Council. He analyzes how media coverage of the Ukraine war may be biased and used to shape public perception, emphasizing the importance of critical analysis and the potential for narrative control by various actors. He argues that a deeper understanding of the underlying factors and motivations is necessary to accurately assess the conflict.

item.title: Google Patches Actively Exploited Chrome Zero-Day (CVE-2026–2441) in CSS Font Processing
item.description:
Google has released an emergency security update to address CVE-2026–2441, the first Chrome zero-day vulnerability reported in 2026. The flaw, rated high severity with a CVSS score of 8.8, was discovered by security researcher Shaheen Fazim and was being actively exploited in the wild. It affects Chrome versions prior to 145.0.7632.75 on Windows and macOS, and 144.0.7559.75 on Linux.
The vulnerability is a Use-After-Free (UAF) memory corruption bug in Chrome’s handling of Cascading Style Sheets (CSS), specifically within the processing of the @font-feature-values rule. In C++, the language used to build much of Chrome’s rendering engine, developers must manually manage memory. A UAF occurs when a program continues to reference memory after it has been freed, potentially allowing attackers to inject malicious data into that memory space.
In this case, Chrome’s internal font feature map could be modified while it was being iterated over, creating a race condition. If an attacker crafte

item.title: Tech Billionaires Limit Their Children’s Screen Time Despite Creating Popular Devices and Platforms
item.description: Many of the world’s leading tech billionaires, including Peter Thiel, Bill Gates, Steve Jobs, Steve Chen, Evan Spiegel, and Elon Musk, are publicly restricting their children’s access to the very technologies and social media platforms they helped develop. Steve Jobs, as early as 2010, said he limited his kids’ use of iPads. Peter Thiel allows his children only 1.5 hours of screen time per week, a practice mirrored by Snap CEO Evan Spiegel. Bill Gates delayed giving his children smartphones until age 14 and banned phones at dinner. Elon Musk acknowledged that not setting rules around social media for his children may have been a mistake. TikTok CEO Shou Zi Chew allows young children to use the app only under the under-13 safety settings, including vetted content and restricted posting. The caution comes amid growing evidence linking excessive screen use and short-form videos to reduced attention spans, poor cognition, and declining mental health in both minors and adults. Recent legisl

item.title: New Jersey Man With No Criminal Record Detained by ICE After Years-Old Deportation Order
item.description:
Abdellatif Hafraoui, a 60-year-old Moroccan immigrant who has lived in the United States for nearly four decades, was detained by ICE for 108 days after being arrested at Newark Liberty International Airport in August 2025. Hafraoui, who has no criminal record, was taken into custody while preparing to board a flight to Florida with his wife, Sandra, a three-time voter for former President Donald Trump. The arrest stemmed from a years-old deportation order issued after an immigration attorney failed to notify Hafraoui of a required court appearance. That attorney was later convicted in a federal immigration fraud scheme.
Hafraoui had previously held valid work authorization and worked as a concierge in Manhattan for nearly 20 years. After marrying Sandra in 2011, he pursued permanent residency through a spousal petition, unaware that the prior removal order remained active. ICE enforced the order, placing him in detention facilities in New Jersey, Louisiana and Arizona. He described h

item.title: Weekly Highlights from Moss & Fog: Art, Design, and Nature
item.description: This week, Moss & Fog’s Fog Signals roundup highlights a diverse mix of visually striking and creative content. Among the most popular posts, a photographer captured an incredible meteor streaking into an active volcano, a shot that combines natural phenomena with cinematic timing. Retro design enthusiasts enjoyed a feature on 1980s-style city logos by Czech designer Kostya Petrenko, bringing back a pixelated, analog aesthetic. Fashion lovers were drawn to Haiti’s Olympic uniforms, designed by Italian-Haitian designer Stella Jean, which showcase vibrant colors and cultural pride. In LEGO news, a new Monet-themed set transforms impressionist paintings into detailed brick constructions, offering a slow, meditative building experience. Architectural innovation was also featured with the Veluwemeer Aqueduct in the Netherlands, a fascinating structure where a road passes beneath a lake while boats sail overhead. Astronomy and nature photography appeared with stunning images of the NEOWISE c

item.title: Millie Bobby Brown Marks 22nd Birthday with David Harbour Amid Past Controversy
item.description: Millie Bobby Brown recently celebrated her 22nd birthday at Maison Close in New York City with a special group of guests, including her husband Jake Bongiovi, 'Stranger Things' co-star David Harbour, and other notable figures from the entertainment world. The event, which took place on a Friday evening, featured Brown in a striking white ruffled dress and her signature elaborate hairstyle. Harbour, who had been involved in a previous controversy surrounding alleged bullying claims by Brown, was seen arriving early and leaving late. The allegations surfaced months before the final season of 'Stranger Things,' where Brown had filed a harassment claim against Harbour. Despite the claims, the two stars appeared to maintain a professional and friendly relationship, posing together at the show's season 5 premiere. The birthday celebration marks a public gesture of unity between the two, with the guest list also including several 'Stranger Things' cast members and close family. The ongoing me

item.title: NATO Faces Internal Tensions and Blame Over Military Spending
item.description: NATO is currently experiencing a significant internal crisis, with member countries openly criticizing each other over military spending. Poland, acting as a voice for the United States, has publicly called on Spain, France, and Italy to increase their defense budgets to meet the commitments made at the last NATO summit in The Hague. Poland’s Defense Minister, Wladyslaw Kosiniak-Kamysz, specifically criticized these three nations for falling behind on military spending, stating, 'We need to do more, faster, and with more strength.' Germany has joined the chorus of criticism, with German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul labeling France’s efforts as insufficient. In response, French Defense Minister Catherine Vautrin emphasized that France has already doubled its defense budget in the last decade and will continue to increase spending. Meanwhile, Poland has emerged as one of NATO’s top contributors, allocating 4.48% of its GDP to defense, compared to around 2% in Spain, France, and Italy

item.title: Bernie Sanders urges urgent action on AI revolution, warns of unpreparedness in the US
item.description: Bernie Sanders has raised concerns about the lack of preparedness in the US regarding the rapid advancement of AI technologies. After meeting with tech leaders in California, Sanders called for immediate policy actions to slow down the development of AI, warning that Congress and the public are not ready for the dramatic changes it will bring. Sanders, alongside congressman Ro Khanna, expressed their fears that AI could exacerbate inequality and lead to mass job losses, particularly in industries such as trucking, fast food, and various white-collar roles. Sanders proposed a moratorium on the expansion of AI data centers as a temporary measure to allow policymakers to catch up.
